name: Boston Dynamics Spot
short_description: A quadruped robot for inspection and data capture in industrial environments.
long_description: A four-legged mobile robot that navigates stairs, rough terrain, and industrial sites carrying sensor payloads. Its animal-like-but-clearly-machine form lands between creature and tool, which shapes how people interpret its presence on a worksite.

images.caption: Boston Dynamics Spot quadruped robot.
images.alt_text: A yellow-and-black Boston Dynamics Spot quadruped robot standing on its four articulated legs.

sources.title: Spot
sources.access_notes: Official product page; commercial availability from 2020.

interactions.title: Walk the plant and flag the hot motor
interactions.description: Spot runs a scheduled route through a facility with a thermal camera payload, reading pixel-level temperatures off pumps and switchgear so a maintenance team catches an overheating motor before it fails.

interactions.title: Let itself through the locked door
interactions.description: On a security patrol Spot uses its arm to turn a handle, and with embedded Bluetooth credentials it can unlock and pass through automated doors on its own, then carry on to the next gauge.

interactions.title: Map radiation no human should walk into
interactions.description: Researchers fitted a collimated radiation sensor to Spot and sent it through Chernobyl's exclusion zone, building a 3D map of contamination while its careful legged gait avoided kicking up radioactive dust.

interactions.title: Herd sheep across a hillside, piloted from another continent
interactions.description: A New Zealand startup put Spot to work mustering a flock over creeks and steep terrain, with a Boston Dynamics team monitoring the run remotely from the United States.

interactions.title: Pull a steampunk rickshaw with its owner aboard
interactions.description: Adam Savage bolted a wheelchair-chassis carriage to Spot's cargo rails and rode behind it, turning an industrial inspection robot into a Victorian-flavored personal cart for a weekend build.

interactions.title: Nail the Running Man on the internet
interactions.description: Boston Dynamics choreographed Spot alongside Atlas and Handle dancing to 'Do You Love Me,' a video that drew tens of millions of views and reframed the machine as charming rather than menacing.

interactions.title: Become a flashpoint for surveillance fears
interactions.description: When the NYPD deployed Spot at a public housing complex, the backlash over policing and militarization was loud enough that the department pulled the robot, an early lesson in how its creature-like form reads as threat in the wrong setting.

interactions.title: Hear a leak before anyone smells it
interactions.description: With an acoustic-imaging payload on its sensor rails, Spot could patrol a compressed-air or gas line and localize a faint hiss to a specific fitting, logging the spot for repair on its next autonomous round.
